threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Calling it 'the Tube' without a hint of sarcasm is a dead giveaway. Locals know it's more of a gamble than a reliable transport option these days.
Last night on the 333, someone brought a saxophone and played 'Careless Whisper' while we waited at the lights. Strange but oddly comforting.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw my neighbor trading homemade sauerkraut for fresh veggies with the guy from two doors down at the corner café.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night Kebab shop that also doubles as an art gallery. The walls are covered in local graffiti, a true cultural mash-up.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to bring a jacket for those 'summer' evenings—even if it’s 75 during the day. The chill creeps in faster than tourists realize.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing guitar while his dog howled along on the 14. Muni is a stage, even when you’re stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all love paella, but locals barely touch it. You’re more likely to find us arguing over the best pixel art in the streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their fascination with every single street performer. Locals just walk by, but newcomers stop for selfies with the saxophonist.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when a bus driver skips a stop because they’re too busy chatting with mates at the front. That’s a classic local move.
Saw a guy on the 59 passionately debating with himself about the best Jerk chicken in Brixton. He almost had a full crowd by the end of it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids playing Fußball with a makeshift goal made of old bicycles in the street. That was the moment I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a cafe that doubles as a record store, with vinyl that plays while your coffee brews. It's a vibe that feels uniquely Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to a BART delay—eye rolls and a quick check for a backup bus route. Tourists just stand there confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Heard a guy on the 19 Polk start a karaoke rendition of 'I Will Survive'—even a few tourists joined in. That’s the Muni spirit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Plaça del Sol is all about the sun; locals know it’s the shade from those big trees that makes it a proper hangout.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still puzzled by the bells at 8 PM, checking their phones instead of enjoying the plaza vibe.
